title: Mode-aware tier floor: floor symlink (120000) and gitlink (160000) changes to T3
acceptance: diff layer carries git object mode/type per changed path (e.g. git diff --raw -z) into a richer diff-entry type, replacing the path-string-only channel into the tier floor
the tier floor floors any changed entry whose new mode is 120000 (symlink) or 160000 (gitlink) to T3 unconditionally, independent of path-glob matching

regression test: a symlink or gitlink at an arbitrary path (e.g. packages/app) that carries reserved/agent-config content floors to T3 even when no path-glob matches; the QRM-3.0 red-team R2/R3 reproductions are covered
BLOCKER: the L2 Gate may NOT be treated as complete tier-floor enforcement until this lands - this is the general counterpart to QRM-3.0's path-based floors

on landing, hand-added bare symlink-directory rules in .quorum/policy.json (**/.claude, **/.codex, **/.cursor, **/.vscode, **/.agents*, bare .github dirs) become redundant and are candidates for retirement (principle 11)

the L2 Gate must run mode-bearing diff (local git diff --raw, or an equivalent mode-carrying GitHubForge.compare) before tier-floor enforcement is treated as real; GitHubForge mode-parity implementation may be deferred but the requirement may not
